news.summary: SEC Commissioner Caroline Crenshaw has criticized the SEC for downplaying risks and misrepresenting the US stablecoin market in its new guidelines. The guidelines exempt stablecoins meeting certain criteria from being considered securities and from transaction reporting requirements. Crenshaw disputes the accuracy of the SEC's analysis, stating that over 90% of USD-stablecoins are available only through intermediaries. The crypto industry sees the guidelines as a positive step, with Token Metrics founder Ian Ballina calling it a focus on what matters in the crypto space. Crenshaw also disagrees with the SEC's assurance that stablecoin issuers can handle unlimited redemptions based on reserve value, stating that stability during market downturns remains a concern. This critique comes after stablecoin issuer Tether announced an audit of its reserves following reports of engaging with a Big Four accounting firm. Some in the industry expressed optimism over the clearer guidance on stablecoins, but Crenshaw insists on the risks involved.
news.headline: SEC criticized for downplaying risks in USD stablecoin market. New guidelines exempt compliant stablecoins from securities status, but criticized as inaccurate by SEC Commissioner. Crypto industry views guidelines positively, while commissioner raises concerns. Over 90% of stablecoins distributed through intermediaries. Progress welcomed, but seen as late by industry. Financial health of stablecoin issuers questioned by SEC Commissioner. Tether reported to undergo asset reserve audit.

news.summary: Shares of Unifirst Corporation (NYSE: UNF) are experiencing a decrease in premarket trading today. Cintas Corporation (NASDAQ: CTAS) recently announced the termination of negotiations to purchase UniFirst at $275 per share in cash. This bid reflected a 46% increase over UniFirst's average closing price for the preceding 90 days leading up to January 6, 2025, the day before the proposal was made public. Todd Schneider, the President of UniFirst, provided further insights...For more details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Cintas withdraws $275 per share offer to acquire UniFirst after talks fail. Offer was a 46% premium over UniFirst's 90-day average closing price as of Jan 6, 2025. UniFirst Corporation shares trading lower premarket. Todd Schneider, President.

news.summary: Pony AI Inc. (PONY) saw a decline in premarket trading after revealing their fourth-quarter results. Sales dropped by 29.8% year over year to $35.52 million, mainly due to the timing of project-based revenue recognition. Revenue from Robotaxi services also decreased by 61.9% Y/Y to $2.6 million, attributed to lower service fees for autonomous vehicle engineering solutions. On the other hand, revenue from Robotruck services increased significantly by 72.7% Y/Y to $12.9 million, driven by expanding robotruck fleet operations into new areas. Gross profit decreased by 56.5% Y/Y to $7.5 million, leading to a margin contraction of 21.0%.
news.headline: Pony AI's Q4 revenue drops by 30% to $35.52 million, robotaxi sales suffer a 61.9% decline, gross profit falls by 56.5%.

news.summary: Adam Hotchkiss, a Goldman Sachs analyst, has reiterated a Buy rating on Waystar Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: WAY) with a price target of $52. Despite a strong performance in 2024, the company's stock has dropped by 14% since its fourth-quarter earnings release, while the S&P 500 saw a 6% decrease during the same period. Investor concerns arose due to a modest uptick in FY25 revenue guidance, indicating a potential slowdown in year-over-year growth. Hotchkiss emphasized these points in his analysis. For further details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Goldman Sachs analyst reiterates Buy rating for Waystar Holding Corp (NASDAQ: WAY) with a $52 price target. Stock fell 14% post strong 2024 performance due to modest FY25 revenue outlook, reflecting growth challenges.

news.summary: Analyst Zach Parham from J.P. Morgan has decided to maintain a Neutral rating on Civitas Resources, Inc. (NYSE:CIVI) and has set a price target of $49 for the stock. Parham's analysis highlights that CIVI is prioritizing debt reduction over share buybacks, staying aligned with its fourth-quarter outlook. The company's primary objective is to achieve a Free Cash Flow (FCF) goal and decrease debt by $800 million by FY25, a target Parham deems feasible at current pricing trends. Furthermore, Civitas Resources is actively pursuing asset sales to reach a target of more than $300 million by FY25. Reports have surfaced indicating CIVI's potential sale of its DJ Basin position for an amount exceeding $4 billion.
news.headline: Civitas Resources is focused on debt paydown and asset sales for FY25 growth, aiming to reduce debt by $800 million and achieve $300 million in asset sales. Neutral rating by J.P. Morgan with a price forecast of $49.

news.summary: Shares of Liberty Energy Inc. (NYSE:LBRT) saw an increase in trading on Thursday following an upgrade from Morgan Stanley analyst Daniel Kutz. The stock was shifted from Equal-Weight to Overweight, with the price forecast also raised from $20 to $25. Kutz's positive outlook is driven by the earnings prospects of the newly established Power Gen Services (PGS) sector. He believes that the company is not being fully recognized for the value of these investments. Additionally, Kutz suggests that the CS business fundamentals may have stabilized, potentially indicating a market floor. This information can be found in the full article on the financial news platform Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Liberty Energy stock (LBRT) rises as Morgan Stanley upgrades from Equal-Weight to Overweight, with price target increasing to $25. Analyst cites earnings potential from PGS business.

news.summary: The article discusses the return of volatility in the markets due to various factors such as trade wars, economic uncertainty, and inflation. Despite this, traders can find opportunities in the market by using the right trading signals. It highlights five stocks that are showing buy signals on indicators like RSI and MACD. One of these stocks is Waste Management Inc. (NYSE:WM), a waste management company with a market cap of over $91 billion and 61,000 employees. WM's stock recently fell to $220 from $234 but bounced back at the 50-day moving average, suggesting a possible support level. The stock is also trading above the 10-day and 20-day moving averages, signaling an uptrend. Additionally, the MACD indicator is indicating a bullish crossover, and the RSI is at 54. WM also has a Benzinga Edge Momentum score of 77.14, indicating potential short-term trading opportunities.
news.headline: Stocks are volatile amid trade wars, 5 trades to consider today with buy signals on RSI & MACD. Waste Management Inc. shows strength at $220, $234, with 50-day moving average support. Benzinga Edge Momentum score: 77.14.

news.summary: On April 4, the US stock market lost $3.25 trillion, exceeding the entire cryptocurrency market's value of $2.68 trillion. The Nasdaq 100 now faces a bear market with a 6% decline. Tesla led with a 10.42% drop. Since February 19, US stocks have erased $11 trillion with over 60% odds of a recession. Trump's tariffs announcement on April 2 could lead to an unavoidable recession. While stocks declined, Bitcoin remained strong at $83,749, down 0.16% in the past week. Even skeptics noted Bitcoin's resilience amid market turmoil. Bitcoin's performance contrasts starkly with stock market trends during global uncertainty.
news.headline: Wall Street's $3.25 trillion loss overshadows entire $2.68 trillion crypto market cap. Nasdaq 100 in 'bear market'. Bitcoin remains strong amidst tradewar fears.

news.summary: In March 2025, the international gold market has shown significant growth, with the COMEX gold futures price surpassing $3,040 per ounce on March 18. Concurrently, the London spot gold price reached $3,030 per ounce, marking new historical highs. The World Gold Council's "Global Gold Demand Trends Report" for 2024 revealed that global central banks have acquired over 1,000 tons of gold annually for the past three years. Data from the People's Bank of China and the State Administration of Foreign Exchange indicate that China's central bank gold reserves have expanded for three consecutive quarters, totaling 73.45 million ounces, underscoring the escalating demand for secure investment assets.
news.headline: Gold prices hit record highs with COMEX futures at $3,040/ounce & London spot at $3,030/ounce. Central banks purchased 1,000 tons of gold in 2024. China's central bank holds 73.45 million ounces.

news.summary: Guardian Capital LP revealed today that the distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund (TSX: GUTB.U) was inaccurately reported in Canadian dollars in the press release from March 17, 2025. As the units of the Fund are in U.S. dollars, the correct distribution is USD$0.1725 per ETF unit. The ex-dividend date, record date, and payment date for the distribution remain the same.
news.headline: Guardian Capital corrected cash distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund to USD$0.1725 per ETF unit.

news.summary: In Edmonton, Alberta, on March 27, 2025, Melcor Real Estate Investment Trust (Melcor REIT) received a favorable recommendation from Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) for the amended and restated plan of arrangement with Melcor Developments Ltd. The arrangement includes redeeming outstanding trust units in exchange for $5.50 per unit. Details of the arrangement can be found in the REIT's management information circular on SEDAR+ and the REIT’s website.
news.headline: ISS recommends Melcor REIT unitholders vote FOR plan of arrangement with MRD. Offers $5.50 per unit.

news.summary: Circle Pharma, a biopharmaceutical company based in South San Francisco, will have its CEO, David Earp, JD, Ph.D., present at upcoming investor conferences. Earp is scheduled to speak at the 24th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference on April 9, 2025, from 11:45 a.m. to 12:25 p.m. ET, followed by the Stifel 2025 Virtual Targeted Oncology Forum from 1:30 p.m. to 1:55 p.m. ET on the same day. The company specializes in developing cell-permeable macrocycle therapeutics.
news.headline: Circle Pharma will present at two virtual conferences on April 9, 2025. CEO David Earp will speak at 11:45 a.m. - 12:25 p.m. ET and 1:30 p.m. - 1:55 p.m. ET.

news.summary: Based in Pacific Palisades, California, Ocampo Capital LLC, a venture firm founded by consumer executive Karl Bracken, recently completed the final close of Ocampo Capital Fund I LP. This fund, targeting early-stage consumer brands and innovative companies in the commerce sector, has successfully secured funding. Ocampo Capital Fund I has already invested in seven companies: Cohere Commerce, Persephone Biosciences, Semaine Health, Small Wonder, Zeya, Future Fans, and Edu.
news.headline: Ocampo Capital Fund I LP closes successfully, targeting early-stage consumer brands. 7 investments made so far.

news.summary: SEC Commissioner Caroline Crenshaw has criticized the SEC for downplaying risks in the US stablecoin market in its new guidelines. She disagreed with the decision to classify stablecoins meeting certain criteria as "non-securities" and exempt from reporting requirements. Crenshaw argued that the SEC's analysis is inaccurate and fails to recognize that over 90% of USD-stablecoins are distributed through intermediaries. Many in the crypto industry, however, see the SEC's decision as a positive step, with some expressing optimism and viewing it as progress in the crypto space. Crenshaw also raised concerns about the SEC's reassurance regarding issuer reserves, stating that stablecoins always carry risk, especially during market stress. This debate comes in light of stablecoin issuer Tether undergoing an audit to verify its backing ratio.
news.headline: SEC's Crenshaw criticizes US stablcoin guidelines for downplaying risks, while industry sees it as progress. Over 90% distributed via secondary market. SEC reassurance on sufficient reserves "grossly inaccurate." Many in industry optimsitic. Stablecoins face risks, especially during market stress.

news.headline: Tether CEO hints at new stablecoin if USDT ousted from US, Europe. USDT may see exit. Paolo Ardoino shares long-term vision.

The WAVLINK Giant Stream6, an AI-Enhanced AX6000 Router, made its debut on Kickstarter and has quickly gained traction in the tech industry. This advanced router boasts impressive speeds and cutting-edge security protocols, providing users with unparalleled performance. Since its launch just two days ago on April 4, 2025, it has garnered significant interest, signaling a promising future for home WiFi technologies.
news.headline: WAVLINK Giant Stream6 WiFi 6 Router gains strong support on Kickstarter, surpassing goal within 48 hours.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) price may surge back to $100,000 faster than expected as it appears to be decoupling from the US stock market and gold. While BTC initially dropped to $82,500 due to President Trump's tariff announcement, it rebounded to $84,700. In contrast, the S&P 500 plunged 10.65%, and gold slipped 4.8%. The anticipated price recovery follows a pattern where gold leads and Bitcoin follows, suggesting significant outperformance by BTC. However, the BTC/XAU ratio is showing a bearish fractal, hinting at potential decline to $65,000 or below if macro conditions worsen. Despite concerns about a US recession due to Trump's tariff war, leading to underperformance of risk assets like Bitcoin, most bond traders expect three rate cuts until September according to CME data. This article does not provide investment advice.
news.headline: Bitcoin traders anticipate rally to $100K as decoupling from stocks and gold trend emerges. Bitcoin bounces from $82,500 to $84,700 while S&P 500 tumbles 10.65%. Gold dropped 4.8%. BTC could follow 2018-2019 trend to hit $100K. Analysts predict a Bitcoin surge over gold. Bitcoin-to-gold ratio suggests drop to $65K, possibly hitting EMA level. US recession fears could hamper Bitcoin bullish outlook. Fed chair signals high-rate environment. Traders betting on three rate cuts by September.
